_schema: A1 canonical N-001 kernel manifest (v1.2). Five physical params read AS-IS by the checker; no reconstruction/hardcoding. delta_{Z,m,r} computed from these stored fields.
tolerance_note: eps_Z, eps_m, eps_r are intentionally equal (1e-9): in the TECT dimensionless energy-scale normalisation all three residuals are dimensionless quantities of the same O(mu2_shell) magnitude class; they are kept as separate fields so a future config may tighten/loosen them independently.
provenance: five params for the N-001 production point (mu2_shell=0.005 shell mass); the verifier reads them AS-IS and calls the vendored N-001 backend (kinetic_coefficients + bloch scalar symbol), with the original solver sources pinned by sha256 and a faithfulness cross-check when the frozen TECT2 tree is reachable.
eta_shell_note: shell-bias coefficient. The N-001 solver symbol is r+Z|k|^2+Y|k|^4+eta_shell*(|k|-q0)^2 (bloch_matrix_linear). The pure Brazovskii A1 kernel REQUIRES eta_shell=0; the verifier enforces it and the stored D_original=r_zero+Z|k|^2+Y|k|^4 holds ONLY under eta_shell=0.
scalar_slice_note: the EXACT params passed to the original bloch_matrix_linear to obtain the pure scalar Brazovskii symbol r+Z|k|^2+Y|k|^4. Each is load-bearing: laplacian_mode!=spectral or bcc_mix_epsilon!=0 changes s2; family_masses!=0 shifts the diagonal; k_lock!=0 (the function DEFAULT is 0.15) adds k_lock*(I-P0); eta_shell!=0 adds the shell-bias (|k|-q0)^2. The verifier reads ALL of these from this JSON (no hardcoding) and passes them to the original function.
backend_kernel_form: D_solver(k)=bloch_matrix_linear(G*={|k|,0,0}, params={r,Z,Y,q0, **scalar_slice})[0,0]; (r,Z)=continuation_mu2_v25.kinetic_coefficients(mu2_shell,Y,q0). Under scalar_slice this reduces to r+Z|k|^2+Y|k|^4 = the stored kernel.
